slides.title: The Basics of CNNs
slides.content:
What makes CNNs special in the world of machine learning?
- Designed to process grid-like data
- Excels in tasks such as image recognition

visual_assets.caption: Basic structure of a CNN showing input, hidden layers, and output.

slides.title: How CNNs Work
slides.content:
CNNs primarily utilize Convolutional and Pooling layers.

- **Convolutional Layer:** Extracts features using filters
- **Pooling Layer:** Reduces dimensionality, retaining important information

visual_assets.caption: Illustration of convolution and pooling operations.

slides.title: Feature Extraction with CNNs
slides.content:
Why use CNNs for feature extraction?
- Captures spatial hierarchies in images
- Reduces need for manual feature engineering

visual_assets.code:
from keras.layers import Conv2D, MaxPooling2D

model.add(Conv2D(32, (3, 3), activation='relu', input_shape=(64, 64, 3)))
visual_assets.caption: An example of defining a Convolutional layer using Keras.

slides.title: CNNs in Action: Image Recognition
slides.content:
CNNs are widely used in image recognition tasks like:
- Object Detection
- Semantic Segmentation

"A picture is worth a thousand words, and CNNs speak fluent picture."

visual_assets.caption: Example results from a CNN-based image recognition system.

slides.title: Beyond Vision: CNN Applications
slides.content:
CNNs aren't just for vision tasks:
- Sound processing
- Anomaly detection
- Natural language processing

visual_assets.caption: Various domains where CNNs are utilized.

slides.title: Common CNN Architectures
slides.content:
- LeNet: One of the earliest convolutional networks
- AlexNet: Popularized deep learning
- ResNet: Introduced residual connections for deeper networks

Understanding these architectures provides insights into evolutionary improvements.

slides.title: Key Takeaways
slides.content:
- CNNs are powerful for grid-like data processing
- Essential in modern computer vision
- Adaptable to various data types beyond images

Remember, experimentation is key to mastering CNNs!
